food_subgroup: Deep-fried potato snacks with cheese and truffle oil

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002670 covers French fries as a food product; no specific code exists for truffle-oil or Grana Padano cheese-topped fries. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers potato products and dishes broadly; no specific facet code for truffle-oil dressed fries with hard cheese topping. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept exists for this specific restaurant snack preparation.

preparation_method: topped with grated Grana Padano cheese

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of truffle and Grana Padano fries is approximately 200 g, presented as a sharing plate of crispy deep-fried potato strips dressed with white truffle oil and finished with grated Grana Padano cheese.

nutrient_source.title: French fries, deep-fried, from fresh (USDA SR Legacy #11408) combined with Grana Padano / Parmesan-type hard cheese (USDA FoodData Central #1100) and white truffle oil adjustment
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy and Foundation Foods)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA or HPB entry exists for truffle-and-Grana-Padano fries. Values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for deep-fried potato strips (~312 kcal/100 g base) with an adjustment for approximately 8 g Grana Padano cheese per 100 g of finished product (USDA FoodData Central hard cheese profile) and a small contribution from white truffle oil (~5 g/100 g). The composite estimate yields approximately 340 kcal/100 g. All macro values are weighted composites from approved USDA sources.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — French fries / fried potato
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 75 is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for deep-fried potato / French fries (GI ~75, glucose reference). Truffle oil and Grana Padano cheese toppings add fat and protein which may modestly lower the effective GI, but the base starch matrix dominates; GI 75 is used as a conservative analogous estimate. GL per serving calculated as (75 × 41.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 200 g serving) / 100 = 61.5.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~320 mg/100 g; ~640 mg per 200 g serving) from salt seasoning and Grana Padano cheese
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to reduce sodium intake, as a 200 g serving contributes approximately 640 mg sodium — about 28% of the 2,300 mg daily limit recommended by major cardiovascular guidelines.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (GI 75) and high carbohydrate load from deep-fried potato base (~41 g carbohydrate/100 g; GL ~62 per serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this dish as an occasional indulgence only; the high GI and high glycemic load of deep-fried potato will produce a rapid postprandial blood glucose rise, and the large serving size amplifies this effect significantly.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Total fat (~17.5 g/100 g) including saturated fat (~4.2 g/100 g) from frying oil and Grana Padano cheese, plus moderate sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this dish due to its combined saturated fat and sodium content; the fat profile from deep-frying and hard cheese contributes to LDL-raising saturated fatty acids, and frequent intake is inconsistent with a heart-healthy dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Truffle and Grana Padano Fries are a premium Italian-inspired snack dish consisting of deep-fried potato strips dressed with white truffle oil and finished with grated Grana Padano DOP hard cheese. The dish is served as a sharing plate in Italian and contemporary European restaurants in Singapore, including at Mingle in Funan Mall. The fries are crispy on the outside and fluffy inside, with the earthy, aromatic character of white truffle oil complementing the salty, umami-rich nuttiness of aged Grana Padano cheese. Grana Padano is a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO) Italian hard cheese similar to Parmigiano-Reggiano but milder and slightly less granular. Nutritionally, the dish is energy-dense at approximately 340 kcal per 100 g, driven by deep-frying in vegetable oil and the fat contribution of the cheese. Carbohydrate content is high (~41 g/100 g) from the potato base, giving the dish a high glycemic index of approximately 75 and a substantial glycemic load per serving. Sodium is moderate (~320 mg/100 g) from seasoning and cheese. The dish is vegetarian but not vegan or dairy-free. It is not suitable for frequent consumption by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease due to its high GI, moderate sodium, and saturated fat content. It is gluten-free in its standard preparation. The dish is priced at SGD 18 at Mingle - Funan and is categorised under snacks in the field survey data platform.
common_user_queries: How many calories in truffle fries?
What is the GI of French fries?
Is Grana Padano the same as Parmesan?
Where to get truffle fries in Singapore?

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific restaurant dish. Nutrient values are estimated composites derived from USDA SR Legacy data for deep-fried potato strips and USDA FoodData Central hard cheese profiles, with a truffle oil adjustment. The GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for deep-fried potato / French fries. Confidence is medium because the estimation methodology is transparent, uses approved sources, and the component foods are well-characterised, but the exact recipe proportions from Mingle - Funan are not publicly available.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source database for this food category. All other -1 fields are not applicable or not reported in the composite source data. Nutrient values are estimated composites; actual values may vary depending on the restaurant's specific recipe, frying oil type, cheese quantity, and portion size. The GI of 75 is an analogous estimate based on deep-fried potato / French fries from the University of Sydney GI Database; the addition of fat and protein from truffle oil and Grana Padano may modestly reduce the effective postprandial glycemic response in practice. Halal status is listed as false because Grana Padano DOP cheese uses animal rennet and the restaurant's halal certification status is not confirmed. Kosher status is not confirmed. The dish is served at Mingle - Funan, Singapore (field survey listing, SGD 18.00).
